name: Trip model: schema migration + sqlc queries
description: Introduce a 'trip' as a derived summary of a route, holding aggregate stats (distance, duration, speeds, engagement, start/end addresses). Every route has at most one trip. This feature only adds the schema and data-access layer -- the aggregation worker, API, and UI are separate features. Why a separate table from routes: routes represent raw ingest state (what segments uploaded); trips represent human-meaningful drive summaries that are recomputed when inputs change.
acceptance_criteria: sql/migrations/003_trips.up.sql creates a trips table: id SERIAL PK, route_id INT UNIQUE NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, distance_meters DOUBLE PRECISION, duration_seconds INT, max_speed_mps DOUBLE PRECISION, avg_speed_mps DOUBLE PRECISION, engaged_seconds INT, start_address TEXT, end_address TEXT, start_lat DOUBLE PRECISION, start_lng DOUBLE PRECISION, end_lat DOUBLE PRECISION, end_lng DOUBLE PRECISION, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ

sql/queries/trips.sql defines sqlc queries: UpsertTrip, GetTripByRouteID, ListTripsByDongleID (paginated, joined with routes to return dongle_id, route_name, start_time), SumTripStatsByDongleID (lifetime totals: total_distance, total_duration, total_engaged, trip_count)
Running sqlc generate produces internal/db/trips.sql.go without errors
The migration applies cleanly against a fresh database: psql -c 'CREATE EXTENSION postgis;' then apply 001, 002, 003
go build ./... and go vet ./... pass cleanly
notes: Migration 003_trips.up/down.sql add a trips table with UNIQUE FK to routes. sqlc queries cover UpsertTrip (ON CONFLICT route_id), GetTripByRouteID, ListTripsByDongleID (paginated join on routes returning dongle_id/route_name/start_time), SumTripStatsByDongleID (lifetime totals). Verified end-to-end against the docker-compose postgis image: CREATE EXTENSION postgis + 001+002+003 apply cleanly, upsert insert/update paths work, ON DELETE CASCADE works, sums return 0 when no trips. go build ./... and go vet ./... pass.
